undecided.label: Soccer Moms
undecided.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
undecided.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

undecided.label: White Working Class
undecided.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

undecided.label: Barstool Conservatives
undecided.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
undecided.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

strongly_support.rationale: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

tooltip.title: Country Club Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

strongly_support.label: Latte Liberals
strongly_support.rationale: The ideological anchor of the Democratic Party. Highly engaged, high-turnout, and the source of most progressive policy energy. When fired up, Democratic campaigns have money and volunteers.

tooltip.title: Latte Liberals
tooltip.subtitle: Democrat, Urban, Graduate/professional degree
tooltip.body: The ideological anchor of the Democratic Party. Highly engaged, high-turnout, and the source of most progressive policy energy. When fired up, Democratic campaigns have money and volunteers.

strongly_support.label: Hispanic Soccer Moms
strongly_support.rationale: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Hispanic, Suburban, College completed
tooltip.body: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

strongly_support.label: Tech Bros
strongly_support.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation, Above median or Rich income
strongly_support.rationale: A new force in campaign finance and online discourse. Mobilized around deregulation, crypto, and anti-establishment messaging. Their money and meme energy can shift narratives quickly.

tooltip.title: Tech Bros
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation, Above median or Rich income
tooltip.body: A new force in campaign finance and online discourse. Mobilized around deregulation, crypto, and anti-establishment messaging. Their money and meme energy can shift narratives quickly.

strongly_support.label: Kennedy Catholics
strongly_support.rationale: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

tooltip.title: Kennedy Catholics
tooltip.subtitle: Catholic, Suburban or Urban
tooltip.body: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

strongly_support.label: Red State Women
strongly_support.demographic_composition: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
strongly_support.rationale: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

tooltip.title: Red State Women
tooltip.subtitle: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

strongly_support.label: Boomer Democrats
strongly_support.demographic_composition: Boomers+, Democrat, College completed or Graduate degree
strongly_support.rationale: The reliable turnout machine of the Democratic Party — they actually show up in midterms. Often overlooked for flashier demographics, but their consistency keeps Democrats competitive in off-year elections.

tooltip.title: Boomer Democrats
tooltip.subtitle: Boomers+, Democrat, College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The reliable turnout machine of the Democratic Party — they actually show up in midterms. Often overlooked for flashier demographics, but their consistency keeps Democrats competitive in off-year elections.

strongly_support.label: Blue-Collar Men
strongly_support.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
strongly_support.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

tooltip.title: Blue-Collar Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

strongly_support.label: GenX Forgotten Middle
strongly_support.rationale: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

tooltip.title: GenX Forgotten Middle
tooltip.subtitle: GenX, Other party affiliation, Suburban
tooltip.body: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

strongly_support.label: Secular Suburbanites
strongly_support.demographic_composition: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
strongly_support.rationale: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

tooltip.title: Secular Suburbanites
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

strongly_support.label: Poor Rural Democrats
strongly_support.rationale: A shrinking but real constituency — complicates the narrative that rural = Republican. Concentrated in the Deep South Black Belt, Appalachian pockets, and Native communities. Mobilization matters in close Senate races.

tooltip.title: Poor Rural Democrats
tooltip.subtitle: Democrat, Rural, Poor (<35k)
tooltip.body: A shrinking but real constituency — complicates the narrative that rural = Republican. Concentrated in the Deep South Black Belt, Appalachian pockets, and Native communities. Mobilization matters in close Senate races.

strongly_support.label: Six-Figure Socialists
strongly_support.rationale: The fundraising engine of the progressive movement. Their policy preferences drive Democratic platform priorities disproportionately. Their disconnect from working-class voters is itself a recurring story.

tooltip.title: Six-Figure Socialists
tooltip.subtitle: Democrat, Urban, Rich (125k+), Graduate/professional degree
tooltip.body: The fundraising engine of the progressive movement. Their policy preferences drive Democratic platform priorities disproportionately. Their disconnect from working-class voters is itself a recurring story.

strongly_support.label: Black Rural Working Class
strongly_support.rationale: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

tooltip.title: Black Rural Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Black - rural - no college
tooltip.body: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

somewhat_support.label: Soccer Moms
somewhat_support.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
somewhat_support.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

somewhat_support.label: White Working Class
somewhat_support.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

somewhat_support.label: Barstool Conservatives
somewhat_support.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
somewhat_support.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives

somewhat_support.label: Disaffected Young Men
somewhat_support.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
somewhat_support.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

tooltip.title: Disaffected Young Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation

strongly_oppose.label: Soccer Moms

strongly_oppose.label: White Working Class

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college

strongly_oppose.label: Barstool Conservatives
strongly_oppose.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
strongly_oppose.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

strongly_support.label: White Working Class

tooltip.title: White Working Class

strongly_support.label: Barstool Conservatives
strongly_support.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
strongly_support.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

somewhat_support.rationale: The reliable turnout machine of the Democratic Party — they actually show up in midterms. Often overlooked for flashier demographics, but their consistency keeps Democrats competitive in off-year elections.

tooltip.title: Boomer Democrats
tooltip.subtitle: Boomers+, Democrat, College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The reliable turnout machine of the Democratic Party — they actually show up in midterms. Often overlooked for flashier demographics, but their consistency keeps Democrats competitive in off-year elections.

somewhat_support.label: Blue-Collar Men
somewhat_support.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
somewhat_support.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

tooltip.title: Blue-Collar Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

somewhat_support.label: GenX Forgotten Middle
somewhat_support.rationale: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

tooltip.title: GenX Forgotten Middle
tooltip.subtitle: GenX, Other party affiliation, Suburban
tooltip.body: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

somewhat_support.label: Secular Suburbanites
somewhat_support.demographic_composition: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
somewhat_support.rationale: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

tooltip.title: Secular Suburbanites
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

somewhat_support.label: Poor Rural Democrats
somewhat_support.rationale: A shrinking but real constituency — complicates the narrative that rural = Republican. Concentrated in the Deep South Black Belt, Appalachian pockets, and Native communities. Mobilization matters in close Senate races.

tooltip.title: Poor Rural Democrats
tooltip.subtitle: Democrat, Rural, Poor (<35k)
tooltip.body: A shrinking but real constituency — complicates the narrative that rural = Republican. Concentrated in the Deep South Black Belt, Appalachian pockets, and Native communities. Mobilization matters in close Senate races.

somewhat_support.label: Six-Figure Socialists
somewhat_support.rationale: The fundraising engine of the progressive movement. Their policy preferences drive Democratic platform priorities disproportionately. Their disconnect from working-class voters is itself a recurring story.

tooltip.title: Six-Figure Socialists
tooltip.subtitle: Democrat, Urban, Rich (125k+), Graduate/professional degree
tooltip.body: The fundraising engine of the progressive movement. Their policy preferences drive Democratic platform priorities disproportionately. Their disconnect from working-class voters is itself a recurring story.

somewhat_support.label: Black Rural Working Class
somewhat_support.rationale: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

tooltip.title: Black Rural Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Black - rural - no college
tooltip.body: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

somewhat_oppose.label: Soccer Moms
somewhat_oppose.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
somewhat_oppose.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

somewhat_oppose.label: White Working Class
somewhat_oppose.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

somewhat_oppose.label: Barstool Conservatives
somewhat_oppose.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
somewhat_oppose.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
tooltip.body: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

somewhat_oppose.label: Disaffected Young Men
somewhat_oppose.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
somewhat_oppose.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

tooltip.title: Disaffected Young Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
tooltip.body: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

somewhat_oppose.label: Black Women of Faith
somewhat_oppose.rationale: The most reliable voting bloc in the Democratic coalition. When their turnout dips even slightly, Democrats lose. Media tracks their enthusiasm as a proxy for the health of the entire Democratic operation.

tooltip.title: Black Women of Faith
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Black, Protestant, Democrat
tooltip.body: The most reliable voting bloc in the Democratic coalition. When their turnout dips even slightly, Democrats lose. Media tracks their enthusiasm as a proxy for the health of the entire Democratic operation.

somewhat_oppose.label: Hispanic Working Class
somewhat_oppose.rationale: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Hispanic, No college
tooltip.body: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

somewhat_oppose.label: Evangelical Republicans
somewhat_oppose.rationale: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

tooltip.title: Evangelical Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Protestant
tooltip.body: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

somewhat_oppose.label: MAGA Country
somewhat_oppose.rationale: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

tooltip.title: MAGA Country
tooltip.subtitle: Rural, Republican, White, No college
tooltip.body: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

somewhat_oppose.label: TikTok Left
somewhat_oppose.rationale: The energy wing of the Democratic base. Their enthusiasm on climate, student debt, and social issues translates to volunteer hours, small-dollar donations, and turnout in college towns. When disengaged, Democratic margins collapse.

tooltip.title: TikTok Left
tooltip.subtitle: Gen Z, Democrat
tooltip.body: The energy wing of the Democratic base. Their enthusiasm on climate, student debt, and social issues translates to volunteer hours, small-dollar donations, and turnout in college towns. When disengaged, Democratic margins collapse.

somewhat_oppose.label: Costco Centrists
somewhat_oppose.rationale: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

tooltip.title: Costco Centrists
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Other party affiliation
tooltip.body: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

somewhat_oppose.label: Tradwives
somewhat_oppose.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
somewhat_oppose.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

somewhat_oppose.rationale: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

tooltip.title: Young Black Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Black, Gen Z

strongly_oppose.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

strongly_oppose.label: GenX Forgotten Middle
strongly_oppose.rationale: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

strongly_oppose.label: Secular Suburbanites
strongly_oppose.demographic_composition: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
strongly_oppose.rationale: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

tooltip.title: Secular Suburbanites

strongly_oppose.label: Poor Rural Democrats
strongly_oppose.rationale: A shrinking but real constituency — complicates the narrative that rural = Republican. Concentrated in the Deep South Black Belt, Appalachian pockets, and Native communities. Mobilization matters in close Senate races.

undecided.label: Soccer Moms
undecided.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
undecided.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

undecided.label: White Working Class
undecided.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

undecided.label: Barstool Conservatives
undecided.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
undecided.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

undecided.label: Disaffected Young Men
undecided.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
undecided.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation

undecided.label: Black Women of Faith
undecided.rationale: The most reliable voting bloc in the Democratic coalition. When their turnout dips even slightly, Democrats lose. Media tracks their enthusiasm as a proxy for the health of the entire Democratic operation.

tooltip.title: Black Women of Faith
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Black, Protestant, Democrat
tooltip.body: The most reliable voting bloc in the Democratic coalition. When their turnout dips even slightly, Democrats lose. Media tracks their enthusiasm as a proxy for the health of the entire Democratic operation.

undecided.label: Hispanic Working Class
undecided.rationale: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Hispanic, No college
tooltip.body: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

undecided.label: Evangelical Republicans
undecided.rationale: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

tooltip.title: Evangelical Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Protestant
tooltip.body: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

undecided.label: MAGA Country
undecided.rationale: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

tooltip.title: MAGA Country

undecided.label: TikTok Left
undecided.rationale: The energy wing of the Democratic base. Their enthusiasm on climate, student debt, and social issues translates to volunteer hours, small-dollar donations, and turnout in college towns. When disengaged, Democratic margins collapse.

tooltip.subtitle: Gen Z, Democrat

undecided.label: Costco Centrists
undecided.rationale: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

tooltip.title: Costco Centrists
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Other party affiliation

undecided.label: Tradwives
undecided.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
undecided.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

key_takeaways: This page shows modeled baseline support for legalizing online casino gaming before respondents saw either scenario.
Use the response-universe toggle to compare the full sample with voters who took a clear position on the issue.
Geography, demographics, and crosstabs show where baseline support and opposition are most concentrated.
content.methodology_note: We estimate a machine learning ensemble that predicts opinion as a function of party, geography, age, sex, education, income, religion, race, and contextual variables. We generate predicted probabilities for detailed demographic cells and post-stratify them to the statewide and cultural-region levels using turnout-adjusted population weights.
